# Feature: AC v2 leave-route parity (Auth/DB evidence bridge)

<!-- auto-generated by /implement on 2026-07-17 — issue #228 -->

## Intent

Wire application-chain analysis so Access Control v2 matrix/inspector shows the same leave Auth/Role/DB truth that legacy `securityMatrix` already has for browo-hr (file-scoped `authenticates`/`validates`/`leave-route-db-fact` edges). No force-green without evidence; demo enrichment stays OFF.

Roadmap: blueprint-access-control (Stufe B) — issue #228

## Preconditions

- SoftwareGraph contains leave routes with file-sourced `authenticates` / `validates` / `data` (`reason: leave-route-db-fact`) edges (as legacy matrix already confirms).
- `VISUDEV_DEMO_ENRICHMENT` is not forced true for verification.
- Unit test harness (Vitest) available under `local-engine`.

## Happy Path

- [ ] Given a leave route whose file node has an `authenticates` edge + auth evidence, `analyzeApplicationChain` emits authentication finding with status `protected` and non-empty `evidence` / mechanisms.
- [ ] Given `leave-route-db-fact` (or same-module data) edges that legacy marks `db=confirmed`, app-chain treats the route as touching data (tenant/ownership/scope not stuck at `not-applicable` solely because BFS missed the file node).
- [ ] Given validates edge on the leave file, validation finding is `protected` with evidence when excerpts/kinds match.
- [ ] Derived `accessControlMatrix` AuthN cell is `protected` for that route; Inspector can show mechanisms + evidence from findings.
- [ ] Legacy `deriveDiagnosticsFromGraph` / `securityMatrix` behavior unchanged for the same fixture.

## Edge Cases

- [ ] Route with no auth/data edges remains `unverified` / `not-applicable` — never invent `protected`.
- [ ] Multi-route file: line-owned evidence still scopes auth/validate signals (reuse `collectRouteEdgeSignals` rules).
- [ ] Truncated chains without file signals stay `unverified` when incomplete.

## Regression

- [ ] Existing app-chain tests (tenant protected, unscoped bypass, non-data route) still pass.
- [ ] Unknown DB adapter still emits `unsupported` for DB-native columns; no RLS criticals invented.
- [ ] Demo enrichment path unchanged (opt-in only).

## Assumptions

- AC v2 has no legacy “DB” column; leave DB confirmation maps to touchesData + resource-scope/tenant/ownership as evidence allows.
- Role/AuthZ stays evidence-driven (`ROLE_RE` / authorize snippets), not force-confirmed from bare auth edges alone when role evidence is absent.

## Implementation Notes

- Files touched: `shared/blueprint-graph-inference.ts` (RouteEdgeSignals + evidence), `local-engine/.../app-chain-reachability.ts` (multi-seed BFS + file seeds), `app-chain-analyzer.service.ts` (bridge collectRouteEdgeSignals), `app-chain-signals.ts` (mergeEvidence)
- Unit tests: leave file-edge → AuthN/AuthZ/Validation protected + evidence; no invent when edges absent; existing chain tests green
- Known limitations: AC v2 has no legacy DB column — leave-route-db-fact sets touchesData; tenant/scope stay missing until tenant/owner evidence exists. Demo enrichment unchanged (OFF).
